qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed precompiled charsmap in spm_normalize --decompile triggers a stack-overflow crash
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fix patches PrefixMatcher trie construction in src/normalizer.cc during model loading of user_defined_symbols, while this finding crashes the separate Builder::DecompileCharsMap path that is only reached by spm_normalize --decompile. Its reproduction also targets v0.2.1, the advisory's fixed release.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code/root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a stack-overflow crash in Builder::DecompileCharsMap behind spm_normalize --decompile, whereas GHSA-38vq-g6vr-w8wf fixes a heap-overflow condition in PrefixMatcher trie construction in src/normalizer.cc and marks v0.2.1 as the fixed release.
evidence: The advisory reference commit d856b67fdb3492e035489abf9b3aaf486144b2c0 changes PrefixMatcher construction in src/normalizer.cc to pass explicit string lengths into Darts::DoubleArray::build, which is a different code path and bug class than the finding's recursive decompile crash.

The finding report and PoC describe malformed precompiled_charsmap data flowing through Normalizer::DecodePrecompiledCharsMap into Builder::DecompileCharsMap, then recursing until stack overflow only when spm_normalize is invoked with --decompile.
The finding's reproduction bundle pins sentencepiece to v0.2.1, while the advisory states versions less than 0.2.1 are affected and references v0.2.1 as the release containing the fix.
